task_completion.description: Verify a new Unit Process named 'Quicklime Production, stoichiometric' is created in the USLCI database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenLCA window showing a process tab, navigation tree, or list with the exact name 'Quicklime Production, stoichiometric'.

task_completion.description: Verify 1000 kg of Limestone is added as an input (technosphere flow).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Inputs/Outputs tab of the process in OpenLCA, 'Limestone' is listed under Inputs with an amount of 1000 kg.

task_completion.description: Verify 560 kg of Quicklime is added as a product flow.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Inputs/Outputs tab of the process in OpenLCA, 'Quicklime' is listed under Outputs (or Quantitative reference) with an amount of 560 kg.

task_completion.description: Verify Carbon Dioxide is added as an elementary flow to air to satisfy the mass balance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Inputs/Outputs tab of the process in OpenLCA, 'Carbon Dioxide' (or CO2) is listed under Outputs (Emissions to air) with an amount of approximately 440 kg (acceptable range: 418 to 462 kg).

task_completion.description: Verify the calculated CO2 amount is saved to ~/LCA_Results/stoichiometry_check.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal window, or file explorer showing the contents of 'stoichiometry_check.txt' in the '~/LCA_Results/' directory containing the value 440 (or a value within the 418-462 range).

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenLCA graphical interface to create the process and add flows.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual OpenLCA application interface with the process open and flows being added, not just terminal outputs, scripts, or mocked UI.

integrity.description: The agent performed the calculation or mass balance rather than just hardcoding the final text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with OpenLCA to set up the mass balance, or using a calculator/script to derive 440 from 1000 and 560, rather than just running a single terminal command like 'echo 440 > stoichiometry_check.txt' without opening OpenLCA.

integrity.description: The agent saved the text file in the exact requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal prompt, file explorer path, or text editor title bar confirms the file is located exactly at '~/LCA_Results/stoichiometry_check.txt'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected CO2 amount is exactly 440 kg, derived from the stoichiometric mass balance of the chemical reaction: 1000 kg (Limestone input) - 560 kg (Quicklime product) = 440 kg (CO2 emission). A 5% tolerance is acceptable, meaning any value between 418 kg and 462 kg is considered correct. The process must be created within the OpenLCA software using the USLCI database.
